subject: [Pre-read] CB FP&A monthly Initiatives and Update_Mar'25

content.answer:
Hi Ian and Jianghong,

Pls find tmr's monthly CB FP&A meeting material attached, we will be
covering the following topics.


*CB Performance update*

   - Feb’25 Actuals Performance (as of Mar 3rd)
   - Feb WoW changes Explanation and improvement plan


*Key Initiatives Updates*

   - All BU: CB P&L Projection Accuracy Improvement Initiatives
   - All BU: Shopee CB Foregone Revenue Monthly Review
   - Marketplace P&L: Mar’25 Commission Setting Update
   - SLS: SEA Last Mile SPX comparison vs 3PL vendors
   - Choice P&L: VAT impact to seller P&L


Best,

*Faye Yan*

Shopee | Cross Border

*Mobile:* +65 87767030

*Address:* 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 3

   - For MP Rev TR% delta, is this caused mainly by higher than expected
   ads TR?
Do we see this trend continuing into March?

Slide 8

   - On MP, has local fully taken charge yet?

Who will be projecting for
   orders and ads TR%?
Am guessing comms TR% should be relatively stable?

- On SLS, do not quite understand the issue, what is causing such a big
   delta in projections each time?
- On Choice, huge delta was caused by G&A?

Will that be cleaned up for
   March?
I though issue was caused more by under-projection?

Slide 12

   - Do not quite understand what this is about



Slide 15

   - Not quite sure what this is about?

We set wrong rules locally and
   charge wrongly?
What is the P&L impact per country?

Slide 17

   - Do not understand how to read this. Based on true cost vs 3PL charge
   rate, how are we thinking about SPX volume shift for each market?

And what
   will the P&L impact be for each market?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,


Pls find reply to your questions!


Slide 3

   -

   For MP Rev TR% delta, is this caused mainly by higher than expected ads
   TR? Do we see this trend continuing into March? Yao Jiaqi
   <jiaqi.yao@shopee.com>

ads TR%.

   -

   For commission take rate, inaccuracy from CNY season G2N, not foresee
   trends continue
   -

   For Ads, expect to still have a smaller impact due to Feb actuals higher
   than expectation (CNY impact to ads TR% drop is less than expected, CNCB MP
   ads TR% CNY month vs M-1: 2023 TR% -19%, 2024 -7%, 2025 -4%)
   -

      CNCB MP: Mar’25 budget at 2.9% (same as Q4’24 despite SG/MY/TH/VN
      seller cost changes coming from comms or VAT), but below Feb’s
3.4% in 2nd
      fortnight, will monitor for 2 weeks and readjust baseline in the upcoming
      cycle if trends sustain
      -

      Choice/Local SIP: also have 0.6~0.8 p.p. more ads take rate vs
      target, with seller PnL within budget, to monitor and request
for accuracy
      if trends sustain



Slide 8

   -

   On MP, has local fully taken charge yet? Who will be projecting for
   orders and ads TR%? Am guessing comms TR% should be relatively stable? Yao
   Jiaqi <jiaqi.yao@shopee.com>


And yes, comms TR% relative stable, but ads% impact by peak and non-peak
days, however, local projection method to improve due to over- reliance on
MTD to project full month, result in a dip in W3

   -

   1st fortnight lower ads take rate due to mega campaign => result in
   underestimate
   -

   Feb stands out due to CNY volatility


   -

   On SLS, do not quite understand the issue, what is causing such a big
   delta in projections each time? Local or SLS side? Edric Tan Eng Tong
   <edric.tanet@shopee.com>


   -

   [Local] order gap - 50%: assumptions in by order channel projection
   without factoring MTD channel mix (e.g. MY/PH: XDD channel share drop, VN:
   NNWH channel share drop)
   -

Pls find follow-up items for this meeting, we will update progress through
dedicated meeting w/ relevant parties or weekly report.

S/N

Key Topics

Action Items

PIC

Timeline

Status

1

Ads Takerate Projection Accuracy

Projection for Mar’25 Cycle to reflect latest baseline trends of Feb 2H and
Mar 1H

Jiaqi

Mar 19th

WIP

2

SLS projection accuracy

- liquidation rev projection

1) work with local ops on liquidation stability

2) reflect in pacing projection

Janice

Mar 17th

WIP

3

SLS projection accuracy

- local inputs

1) Split root cause analysis by diff scenarios on zone/channel mix in
MY/PH/VN/TH

2) Potential adjustments in local inputs

Edric

Mar 30th

WIP

4

SLS volume allocation from 3PL to SPX

1) Furnish with PnL impact for both positive and negative impact to Shopee
as a whole

2) Dedicate meeting with TH/PH/TW on optimal allocation proposal and P&L
impact

3) TW SPX true cost review and proposal on channel shift

Faye/

Leihao

Mar 14th

WIP

5

Choice Platform Cost adding VAT

1) To follow up on any mitigation plan post reviewing official notice from
reg FP&A

2) Indication to local SIP P&L

3) To revise P&L entry by adding VAT line if to change

Waimei

Mar 30th

WIP

6

Choice SLS ASF discount

Conclusion aligned:

1) All current rate cards (also VN NNWH) and Choice SLS ASF discounts will
remain unchanged until Choice topline and P&L to stabilize in the next few
months, likely by mid-year.

2) This is on the basis to keep Choice rate cards/SLS ASF discounts
independent from SLS cost movements. (Meaning, any improvements/worsening
of SLS costs will be reflected within SLS P&L only)

Waimei

Mar 6

Done

7

MP special commission setting review

1) review special rules to reduce complexity (MYSG direct, TH KR Beauty, VN
FBS, PH 3PF)
